word: G578 - ἀποβλέπω - apoblépō - ap-ob-lep'-o - Have respect

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀποβλέπω

- Transliteration: Apoblepo

- Phonetic: ap-ob-lep'-o

- Definition:

1. to turn the eyes away from other things and fix them on some one thing

a. to look at attentively

2. to look with steadfast mental gaze

- Origin: from G575 and G991

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G575 and G991; to look away from everything else that is (figuratively) intently regard: - have respect.
